overview.introduction: Docker Networking isolates, connects, and controls container network interfaces. By managing driver types, port rules, and user-defined bridge networks, you enable secure container communications.
overview.why_it_matters: By default, Docker places all containers on a shared bridge network. However, this default network does not support container name DNS resolutions, leaving you to hardcode IPs. Custom networks solve this by providing isolated namespaces and built-in name lookup services.
overview.industry_usage: Used by site reliability and DevOps teams to set up private database backends, configure reverse proxy gateways (like Nginx/Traefik), and establish strict service isolation rules.
overview.what_you_will_build: A custom bridge network, launch frontend and backend containers sharing the namespace, run inter-container lookup pings, and clean up active interfaces.
learning_outcomes: Identify active driver networks using CLI listings.
Create custom bridge networks specifying custom drivers.
Query driver subnets and IP pools using network inspects.
Run containers explicitly assigned to custom networks.
Test DNS name lookups between container sandboxes.
Dismantle and remove active network configurations.

key_concepts.title: Network Driver Types
key_concepts.definition: Docker uses pluggable drivers to manage network topologies. Standard types include: Bridge (private network on host), Host (uses host network directly), and None (isolated network loopback).
key_concepts.explanation: Bridge is default for standalone containers. Host driver eliminates routing overhead for high throughput. None blocks all outward card connectivity for absolute safety.

commands.name: docker network ls
commands.expected_output:
NETWORK ID     NAME      DRIVER    SCOPE
112233445566   bridge    bridge    local
223344556677   host      host      local
334455667788   none      null      local
commands.output_explanation: Lists the default driver layers loaded in docker daemon.

key_concepts.title: Built-in DNS Name Resolution
key_concepts.definition: Containers on user-defined custom networks can communicate with each other using container names as host addresses.
key_concepts.explanation: Docker runs a DNS service at address 127.0.11. When container A looks up container name 'backend_app', Docker resolves it to its actual dynamic bridge IP address automatically.

commands.name: docker network inspect
commands.purpose: View configuration details of a network.
commands.output_explanation: Prints driver details, subnets, and connected container interfaces in JSON layout.

lessons.title: 1. List Existing Networks
lessons.definition: Auditing active networking layers on the local Docker daemon.
lessons.explanation: Inspect default adapters before adding custom routing interfaces.
lessons.instruction: Print active network list using docker network ls.

lessons.title: 2. Create Custom Bridge Network
lessons.definition: Initializing a user-defined isolated bridge network adapter.
lessons.instruction: Create a custom network named 'custom_bridge' using the network command.

lessons.title: 3. Inspect Custom Network Details
lessons.definition: Querying address spacing, subnets, and scopes of custom networks.
lessons.instruction: Display structural metadata of custom_bridge using docker network inspect.

lessons.title: 4. Launch Redis on Custom Network
lessons.definition: Starting database services directly inside custom networking blocks.
lessons.instruction: Run a detached Redis container named 'backend_app' on 'custom_bridge'.
lessons.hint: Type: docker run -d --name backend_app --network custom_bridge redis

lessons.title: 5. Launch Client Container
lessons.definition: Running client sandboxes connected to the same communication network.
lessons.instruction: Run a detached Alpine container named 'frontend_app' sleeping for 1 hour on 'custom_bridge'.
lessons.example: docker run -d --name frontend_app --network custom_bridge alpine sleep 3600
lessons.hint: Type: docker run -d --name frontend_app --network custom_bridge alpine sleep 3600
lessons.solution: docker run -d --name frontend_app --network custom_bridge alpine sleep 3600

lessons.title: 6. Verify Container DNS Resolution
lessons.definition: Testing name-based connection lookup between isolated containers.
lessons.instruction: Run a 3-packet ping from frontend_app to backend_app using docker exec.
lessons.hint: Type: docker exec frontend_app ping -c 3 backend_app

lessons.title: 7. Stop and Delete Database Service
lessons.definition: Halting container runtime processes and clearing active definitions.
lessons.instruction: Stop and remove the backend_app container to clean resources.
lessons.example: docker stop backend_app && docker rm backend_app
lessons.hint: Type: docker stop backend_app && docker rm backend_app
lessons.solution: docker stop backend_app && docker rm backend_app

lessons.title: 8. Remove Custom Network
lessons.definition: Clearing unused bridge networks from system memory cache.

exercises.title: Connect Running Container
exercises.problem: Launch a detached container on default network, then use `docker network connect` to attach it to custom_bridge dynamically.
exercises.hint: docker run -d --name dynamic_runner nginx, then docker network connect custom_bridge dynamic_runner.
exercises.expected_result: inspecting custom_bridge lists dynamic_runner as a connected container.
exercises.objective: Practice dynamic network binding without restarting containers.

exercises.title: Inspect Host Port Bindings
exercises.problem: Run a web server forwarding host port 9000 to port 80, and verify its binding mapping in network inspect.
exercises.hint: docker run -d -p 9000:80 nginx, then check Ports in docker ps.
exercises.objective: Understand public port mappings on Docker hosts.

quiz.question: Which network driver is the default for standalone containers?
quiz.explanation: By default, Docker assigns standalone containers to the 'bridge' network adapter.

quiz.question: Which network driver shares the host machine's IP address directly?
quiz.explanation: The 'host' driver runs container interfaces directly on the host stack, avoiding NAT overhead.

quiz.question: What is the primary benefit of custom user-defined networks over default networks?
options: They support built-in DNS name resolution between containers.
quiz.answer: They support built-in DNS name resolution between containers.
quiz.explanation: Containers on user-defined networks can ping each other using container name aliases, which default bridge networks block.

quiz.question: How do you view which containers are attached to a network?
quiz.explanation: 'docker network inspect [network]' displays IP allocations, routes, and connected container hashes in JSON format.

quiz.question: Which CLI command creates a network named 'frontend'?
quiz.explanation: 'docker network create frontend' initiates a standard local bridge network named frontend.

quiz.question: What does network driver 'none' provide?
options: Unrestricted access to all public networks.
No network interface for the container except local loopback.
quiz.answer: No network interface for the container except local loopback.
quiz.explanation: Driver 'none' isolates container namespaces completely, blocking both ingress and egress traffic.

quiz.question: How do you connect an already running container 'my_app' to a network 'my_net'?
quiz.explanation: 'docker network connect [network] [container]' dynamically binds container interfaces without stopping running processes.

quiz.question: Which network driver type enables multi-host container networks in Kubernetes/Swarm?
quiz.explanation: The 'overlay' driver establishes secure virtual routing spans across multiple distinct host nodes.

quiz.question: How do you remove a network from the Docker system catalog?
quiz.explanation: 'docker network rm [network]' deletes custom networks once all connected containers are unlinked.

quiz.question: What happens if you run two containers with the same name on the same network?
options: Docker resolves name conflicts using suffixes.
Docker daemon blocks the second startup with a name conflict error.
The first container is terminated immediately.
quiz.answer: Docker daemon blocks the second startup with a name conflict error.
quiz.explanation: Container names must be globally unique per daemon socket to prevent identification conflicts.

resources.title: Docker Network Drivers Documentation
resources.description: Guides detailing bridge, host, none, macvlan, and overlay driver setups.

resources.title: Docker Port Mappings Reference
resources.description: Guide explaining container port publication, address bindings, and iptables rules.
